reasoning: Spain is the market favorite and the best team on the evidence so far, carrying a historic winning streak, elite depth built around Rodri, Pedri and Lamine Yamal, and reigning-champion pedigree from Euro 2024. France remain the bookmakers' other clear pick with the deepest squad, calm knockout pedigree under Deschamps and a relatively kind bracket path, while defending champions Argentina — powered by Messi's scoring surge — are the most dangerous obstacle and my projected runner-up. England are the strongest of the next tier, and Brazil and Portugal loom in Spain's half as genuine threats. Morocco at long odds is the live outsider, having reached the final eight again and proven capable of upsetting elite European sides, though the altitude/heat edge favors the Latin American and warm-climate teams overall.
